idea: Explain the nb99 Socratic agentic method as an engineered process for turning cross-model disagreement into a more trustworthy educational artifact, while showing that the human remains responsible for the contract, scope, reopening decisions, and publication.
must_avoid: Do not describe model agreement as proof of truth.
Do not claim that product_i11 has two-model convergence or an Opus signoff.
Do not call FABLE5 a party to the frozen two-model contract.
Do not reduce the essay to a repository tour, setup guide, or list of features.
Do not turn the ending into a product pitch.
Do not imitate lowercase, fragments, favorite terms, or dramatic cadence mechanically.
Do not invent test results, citations, model capabilities, or causal performance claims.

claims: The project merged a rigorous, self-check-heavy notebook and a build-until-it-breaks narrative notebook into one NumPy-based course.
The frozen human contract defined six auditable axes and made external mathematical truth outrank both source notebooks.
The workflow alternated audit, meta-audit, rework, and signoff, with the filesystem serving as the durable coordination layer.
Formal two-model zero-waiver convergence occurred on product_i7 after 33 findings, an unchanged verification round, and signatures from both models on the same artifact.
The process included consequential reopenings and a retracted premature signoff, showing why resolved findings and signoffs were not treated as irrevocable truth.
A later human-commissioned FABLE5 audit found pedagogical scar tissue and prompted a bounded recomposition phase outside the original contract loop.
The final product_i11 contains 128 cells, including 48 executable code cells and 16 embedded figures, and passed Sol's independent final checks with a zero-waiver signoff.
The historical Opus signoff still names product_i7, so product_i11 must not be described as satisfying the original two-model convergence predicate.
The reusable method is a governance pattern for bounded artifact review, not evidence that model consensus guarantees truth.
required_movements: Open with the filesystem baton or a ledger row as the concrete carrier.
Explain why merging the two source notebooks created conflicts that ordinary drafting could hide.
Show how the contract, roles, ledger states, frozen fixes, and executable checks made disagreement operational.
Use at least two concrete failure-and-reopening episodes to show that the method could correct its own premature confidence.
Move from the formal i7 convergence to the human decision to reopen pedagogy and the bounded path to i11.
State the exact status boundary between i7 and i11.
Extract the smallest reusable engineering pattern without presenting the project as a universal benchmark.
End with human judgment as the authority that writes the contract, changes the scope, and decides whether the artifact is ready to publish.
uncertainties: The archive demonstrates this method on one notebook-convergence project; it does not establish comparative performance against other review protocols.
A clean execution and a closed ledger establish compliance with the chosen contract, not universal correctness or pedagogical perfection.

requested_register: technical expository with reflective first-person implication
